data.narrative: Student successfully implemented production-ready deployment configuration with Dockerfile (0.0.0.0 binding, PORT env var), server.js with /health and /ready endpoints, graceful shutdown hooks (SIGTERM/SIGINT with 10s timeout), and GitHub Actions CI workflow. Chose single container deployment for operational simplicity. Fixed port mismatch bug by restoring process.env.PORT reading. Strong understanding of production deployment patterns, health checks, readiness checks, and graceful shutdown.

dimension_scores.observation: Chose single container deployment for operational simplicity in bootcamp environment, accepting that every failure is total but rollback is simpler.
dimension_scores.evidence: Chose single container because analytics queue uses mock BullMQ implementation, no actual separate worker process. Reduces operational complexity - no inter-service communication, service discovery, or separate deployment pipelines. Tradeoff: every failure is total but rollback is simpler (redeploy previous version).

dimension_scores.observation: Injected port mismatch bug by hardcoding PORT, reproduced symptom (service ignores Railway PORT env var), fixed by restoring process.env.PORT reading.
dimension_scores.evidence: Bug injection in src/server.js line 10 changed from const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000 to const PORT = 3000. Symptom: service ignores Railway PORT env var, binds to wrong port, traffic fails. Fix restored const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000.

dimension_scores.observation: Implemented environment validation rules to prevent tests from hitting production database, documented Knight Capital incident as cautionary example.
dimension_scores.evidence: Production safety rule: assert DATABASE_URL contains 'test' or 'localhost' before connecting. Prevents Knight Capital scenario where wrong env var caused production data changes in 45 minutes ($440M loss).

dimension_scores.observation: Implemented health check (server process only), readiness check (dependencies ready), graceful shutdown (10s timeout, drain connections), proper timeouts (health check 30s interval, 3s timeout).
dimension_scores.evidence: Health endpoint returns 200 if server running, NOT dependencies to prevent cascade failures. Readiness endpoint returns 200 only if dependencies ready, 503 if not ready or shutting down. Graceful shutdown handles SIGTERM/SIGINT with 10s timeout, server.close() drains connections.

dimension_scores.observation: Provided clear explanation of deployment strategy, operational lessons, health vs readiness differences, and rollback plans.
dimension_scores.evidence: Explained single container choice, operational lesson (PORT env var critical), health vs readiness (health checks server only, readiness checks dependencies), rollback plan (re-deploy last known-good, restore DB from backup), build-time vs runtime config differences.

strengths: Strong understanding of production deployment patterns and platform-specific requirements
Excellent debugging methodology identifying and fixing port configuration bug
Clear production thinking with health checks, readiness checks, and graceful shutdown implementation
data.recommendation: Bootcamp complete. Student has demonstrated strong understanding of system design fundamentals across all 10 modules.

public_evidence.student_action: Created Dockerfile with 0.0.0.0 binding, PORT env var configuration, healthcheck with 30s interval and 3s timeout.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Dockerfile at root with Node.js 18-alpine base, WORKDIR /app, npm ci --only=production, COPY . ., EXPOSE 3000, ENV NODE_ENV=production PORT=3000, HEALTHCHECK with 30s interval 3s timeout 5s start-period 3 retries, CMD node src/server.js.
public_evidence.proof: Dockerfile binds to 0.0.0.0 for platform routing, PORT configurable via env var, healthcheck configured to verify server health. Server.js reads PORT from process.env.PORT || 3000, binds to 0.0.0.0, implements /health and /ready endpoints.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows understanding of Docker best practices, platform-specific requirements (0.0.0.0 binding, PORT env var), and health check configuration.

public_evidence.student_action: Implemented /health endpoint (server process check) and /ready endpoint (dependencies check) with graceful shutdown support.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Server.js at src/server.js with healthCheck function (returns 200 if server running), readinessCheck function (returns 200 if dependencies ready, 503 if dbConnected=false or isShuttingDown=true), gracefulShutdown function (SIGTERM/SIGINT with 10s timeout, server.close() drains connections).
public_evidence.proof: /health returns {status:healthy, timestamp} always if server running. /ready returns {status:ready, timestamp} if dbConnected=true, 503 if dbConnected=false or isShuttingDown=true. Graceful shutdown logs 'Received SIGTERM, starting graceful shutdown...', closes server, exits after 10s timeout.
public_evidence.ability: Implements health and readiness checks with graceful shutdown
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Demonstrates understanding of health vs readiness separation, dependency checking, and graceful shutdown patterns for production deployments.

public_evidence.student_action: Created CI workflow running npm ci and npm test on push/PR to main branch with Node.js 18 setup.
public_evidence.technical_detail: CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml triggers on pull_request and push to main. Runs on ubuntu-latest, uses actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4 with node-version 18 and npm cache, runs npm ci and npm test.
public_evidence.proof: Workflow runs on every push and PR to main, sets up Node.js 18 with npm caching for faster installs, installs dependencies with npm ci, runs tests with npm test. Ensures code changes are tested before merge.
public_evidence.ability: Implements CI pipeline with automated testing
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows understanding of CI/CD best practices, automated testing on every change, and proper workflow configuration.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Implemented Dockerfile with 0.0.0.0 binding, PORT env var, healthcheck. Server.js with /health, /ready, graceful shutdown. GitHub Actions CI workflow.
Health check verifies server process only (lightweight, no dependencies). Readiness check verifies dependencies ready (DB/Redis/queue) before platform sends traffic.
Implemented SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers with 10s timeout, server.close() to drain connections, process.exit(0) after in-flight requests complete.
Fixed port mismatch bug by restoring process.env.PORT reading. Explained build-time vs runtime config differences. Documented production safety rules.

teaching_approaches.detail: Provided concrete implementation of Dockerfile, server.js with health/ready endpoints, graceful shutdown, and CI workflow.
